channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Russia, Ukraine take 'significant first step toward peace' after Rubio-led negotiations, White House insists
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said that the White House is "ensuring all parties are heard" as negotiations for a peace deal kick off to end the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy faces perilous re-election odds as US, Russia push Ukraine to go to the polls as part of peace deal
item.description: With Ukranian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's first term more than a year past expired, the U.S. and Russia are both pressuring the country to hold new elections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge questions motives for Trump's order banning transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday questioned President Donald Trump's motives for issuing an executive order that calls for banning transgender troops from serving in the U.S. military, describing a portion of the directive as “frankly ridiculous.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge declines to immediately block Elon Musk or DOGE from federal data or layoffs
item.description: A federal judge declined Tuesday to immediately block Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency from accessing government data systems or participating in worker layoffs.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supervisor in DC federal prosecutors' office told to resign after dispute over investigation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top supervisor in the federal prosecutors' office in Washington said she was forced to resign following a dispute with her boss over a directive that she scrutinize the awarding of a government contract during the Biden administration, according to a letter reviewed by The Associ
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge denies Democrat-led effort to block DOGE access, citing lack of proven harm
item.description: A Wasington, D.C.-based federal judge has declined to block billionaire Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing government data or firing federal employees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court should keep whistleblower agency head in place for now, lawyers say in reply to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court should reject an emergency appeal from the Trump administration asking for quick action to allow the firing of the head of the federal agency that protects whistleblowers, lawyers for the embattled official told the justices on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Louisiana surgeon general wants to 'depoliticize medicine' by ending statewide mass vaccinations
item.description: After directing staff at the Louisiana Department of Health to halt all mass vaccination programs, Louisiana Surgeon General Dr. Ralph Abraham speaks to Fox News Digital about the criticisms his move has faced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US GM Bill Guerin says he would welcome Trump when Americans play Canada for 4 Nations title
item.description: The general manager of the U.S. team in the 4 Nations Face-Off hockey tournament said he would welcome a visit from President Donald Trump to the championship game between the United States and Canada on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court rules Republican had no right to bring lawsuit challenging mobile voting
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A divided Wisconsin Supreme Court ruled Tuesday that a Republican Party official lacked the standing to bring a lawsuit challenging the use of a mobile voting van in 2022.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The USAID shutdown is upending livelihoods for nonprofit workers, farmers and other Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There's the executive in a U.S. supply-chain company whose voice breaks while facing the next round of calls telling employees they no longer have jobs. And a farmer in Missouri who grew up knowing that a world with more hungry people is a world that’s more dangerous.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court rules Republican had no right to bring lawsuit challenging mobile voting
item.description: A divided Wisconsin Supreme Court ruled Tuesday that a Republican Party official lacked the standing to bring a lawsuit challenging the use of a mobile voting van in 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: White House says Elon Musk is not in charge at DOGE, but is advising the president
item.description: The White House says billionaire Elon Musk is not the administrator of the Department of Government Efficiency team sweeping through federal agencies, but rather a senior adviser to President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Retail recreational marijuana, take two. Cannabis management leader speaks to rollout
item.description: On Tuesday, the Office of Cannabis Management started accepting applications for licenses to grow and sell marijuana — for a second time. Eric Taubel, interim director, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Red state lawmakers look to punish local governments defying Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: The governor of Indiana would be able to punish local government agencies that fail to comply with federal immigration law under a bill working through the state legislature.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico’s President Sheinbaum threatens to take legal action over the Gulf name change
item.description: Earlier this month, Sheinbaum shared a letter addressed to Google with reporters, arguing that the U.S. had no authority to unilaterally rename the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Manuela López Restrepo, MPR News

item.title: House, Senate standoff over Trump agenda escalates
item.description: The showdown between House and Senate Republicans is escalating as the two groups barrel ahead with their contrasting strategies to pass President Trump’s legislative agenda — with the upper chamber expected to move this week.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NAACP guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The advisory also lists companies that have recommitted to DEI in recent weeks – such as Delta Airlines, Apple and Ben & Jerry’s.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. rescinds Indian Health Service layoffs
item.description: The last-minute decision to rescind the layoff order for IHS came as health care workers waited for bad news.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: GOP lawmakers divided on Musk, seen by some as a liability
item.description: Republicans on Capitol Hill are divided over tech billionaire Elon Musk being the public face of President Trump’s aggressive bid to shrink the government, with some souring on Musk’s prominent role as he increasingly has become a target of Democratic attacks.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump officials signal potential changes at NOAA, the weather and climate agency
item.description: Federal workers at the home agency for the National Weather Service are concerned about a potential overhaul by Trump officials focused on cutting government costs.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda, Michael Copley, and Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: PIX on Politics Panel breaks down Adams-DOJ controversy
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It’s been a politically charged week for Mayor Eric Adams after the Department of Justice ordered prosecutors to drop a federal corruption case against him.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Judge blocks Trump’s gender-affirming care executive orders nationwide
item.description: BALTIMORE — A federal judge Thursday blocked parts of two executive orders issued by President Trump that collectively seek to restrict gender-affirming care. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's health secretary after close Senate vote
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as President Donald Trump’s health secretary, putting the prominent vaccine skeptic in control of $1.7 trillion in federal spending, vaccine recommendations and food safety as well as health insurance programs for roughly half the country.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kamala Harris would be Democratic frontrunner for California governor in 2026, poll suggests
item.description: Former Vice President Harris would be the Democratic frontrunner for governor of California if she jumped into the Golden State’s 2026 race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom (D), new polling shows.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WFLA, NBC
